capabilities.notes: Spec 3.3B fake backend proves the backend contract for dense_logits. The active public builder has not migrated to this backend contract yet.
Active fake teacher mode can write top-k plus tail targets through the current NumPy builder path.
Active fake teacher mode can write cascaded soft-label targets through the current NumPy builder path.
Fake dynamic cascaded soft-label backend emission is not implemented in Spec 3.3F6; the CPU reference backend owns the contract shape.
Fake corridor/exemplar generation exists elsewhere as synthetic proof work, but not as a runtime backend target policy.
Spec 3.3C CPU reference backend emits deterministic dense logits through the backend contract for debug/reference use. The public builder has not migrated to this backend.
Spec 3.3C CPU reference backend emits deterministic top-k plus tail payloads through the backend contract.
Spec 3.3C CPU reference backend emits deterministic cascaded soft-label payloads through the backend contract.
Spec 3.3F6 CPU reference backend emits dynamic top-k explicit head plus bucketed tail payloads with padded mask-driven shape.

Spec 3.3F8 CPU reference corridor_exemplar_v1 emits the locked production schema with source-policy-aware metadata. It remains unoptimized CPU reference behavior. Spec 3.3F9.1 records one_pass_candidate capture mode metadata. Spec 3.3F9.2 adds explicit two_pass_sparse_exemplar score_pass summaries and selected_exemplar_pass production helper support. Spec 3.3F9.3 adds auto capture selection with explicit byte estimates and auto_policy_reason metadata. Spec 3.3F9.4 adds GPU batch-size policy/guardrail metadata without target-policy support changes. Spec 3.3F10 routes backend emissions through the builder and preserves metadata without changing support status.

Spec 3.3E HF Torch backend emits real causal-LM dense logits through the backend contract when optional local HF dependencies and model files are available. The public builder has not migrated to this backend.
Spec 3.3E HF Torch backend emits top-k plus tail payloads by CPU-reducing real HF logits through the backend contract.
Spec 3.3E HF Torch backend emits cascaded soft-label payloads by CPU-reducing real HF logits through the backend contract.
HF Torch dynamic cascaded soft-label support is planned after the Spec 3.3F6 CPU reference contract shape.
HF Torch corridor/exemplar target policy remains planned after the F8 production schema lock; no HF corridor emission is implemented now.

Spec 3.3F1 gpu_torch emits dense debug HF logits on an available CUDA or MPS Torch accelerator. This path is not optimized and transfers dense logits back to host. Spec 3.3F5 adds structured diagnostics and no backend-local CPU fallback.

Spec 3.3F2 gpu_torch computes GPU compact top-k/tail reduction on CUDA or MPS and transfers only compact payload arrays back to host. Spec 3.3F4 adds optional vocab chunking and deterministic memory/workspace metadata. Spec 3.3F5 hardens runtime diagnostics and fallback semantics.

Spec 3.3F3 gpu_torch computes GPU compact cascaded soft-label reduction on CUDA or MPS, including bucket masses, and transfers only compact payload arrays back to host. Spec 3.3F4 adds memory metadata and shared probability workspace reuse. Spec 3.3F4.1 records requested cascaded chunking honestly when exact bucket construction requires a full probability workspace. Spec 3.3F5 adds structured runtime diagnostics.

Spec 3.3F7 gpu_torch computes GPU dynamic top-k explicit head plus bucketed tail on CUDA or MPS and transfers only compact payload arrays back to host. Spec 3.3F10 routes gpu_torch builder emissions through the backend contract with metadata propagation and no silent CPU fallback.

Spec 3.3F9 gpu_torch implements optimized corridor_exemplar_v1 emission against the F8 production schema, supports dense, fixed cascaded, and dynamic cascaded exemplar source policies, and transfers only compact production arrays back to host. Spec 3.3F9.1 formalizes this implementation as one_pass_candidate capture mode. Spec 3.3F9.2 adds explicit two_pass_sparse_exemplar score_pass reduction and selected_exemplar_pass helper support. Spec 3.3F9.3 adds auto capture selection with explicit byte estimates and auto_policy_reason metadata. Spec 3.3F9.4 adds GPU batch-size policy/guardrail metadata without target-policy support changes. Spec 3.3F10 routes gpu_torch builder emissions through the backend contract with metadata propagation and no silent CPU fallback.
TPU dense logits are planned for debug/reference shape only; no active TPU implementation exists.
TPU top-k plus tail support is planned and must be designed without CUDA assumptions.
TPU cascaded soft-label support is planned and must be designed around JAX/XLA constraints.
TPU dynamic cascaded soft-label support is planned after CPU/GPU contract and reducer work; no active TPU implementation exists.
TPU corridor/exemplar support is planned as a future backend family after the F8 production schema lock, not active implementation.

non_goals: Do not migrate the public builder to hf_torch or gpu_torch in Spec 3.3F7.
Do not claim staged mode is performance optimized in Spec 3.3F7.
Do not claim measured peak GPU memory or OOM recovery in Spec 3.3F7.
Do not silently fall back to CPU when gpu_torch is explicitly requested.
Do not implement backend-local CPU fallback when gpu_torch fallback_policy is auto.
Do not implement corridor/exemplar production schema or GPU corridor/exemplar acceleration in Spec 3.3F7.
Do not implement TPU/JAX backend code in Spec 3.3F7.
Do not change target shard binary layout, cover page schema, or .rtome bundle format in Spec 3.3F7.

orchestration.notes: Spec 3.3D adds deterministic CPU-side backend batch orchestration. Staged is pipeline-shaped but not yet a historical optimizer port.
